data.title: File Format Foundation
data.body:
## Scope
Low-level binary parsing of SDF file header and page structure.

## Context
SDF files use magic bytes `EB 6D 87 AE`, 4096-byte pages, little-endian encoding.
Sample file: `data/Depropanizer.sdf` (5,001,216 bytes = 1221 pages).

## Key Decisions
- Page size: 4096 bytes (verified from sample file)
- All integers are little-endian
- Header fields verified: magic(0x00), LCID 1033(0x28), version 4(0x30)

data.title: Parse SDF file header
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `format/header.go` with `FileHeader` struct and `ReadHeader(io.ReaderAt) (*FileHeader, error)`
- Parse and validate: magic bytes (`EB 6D 87 AE`), page size, DB version, LCID, encryption flag
- Create `format/constants.go` with magic, version, and type ID constants

## Acceptance Criteria
- `ReadHeader` returns error for non-SDF files
- `ReadHeader` successfully parses `data/Depropanizer.sdf` header
- Magic = `0xAE876DEB`, version = 4, LCID = 1033

## Validation Gates
- `go build ./format/...`
- `go test ./format/... -run TestReadHeader`

data.title: Page-level I/O reader
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `format/page.go` with `PageReader` struct
- Read any page by number: `ReadPage(pageNum int) ([]byte, error)` where offset = pageNum * pageSize
- LRU cache for recently accessed pages (configurable size)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can read any page 0-1220 from Depropanizer.sdf
- Page 0 starts with magic bytes
- Cache hit avoids re-reading from disk

## Validation Gates
- `go build ./format/...`
- `go test ./format/... -run TestPageReader`

data.title: Classify page types
data.body:
## Goal
- Add page type classification to `format/page.go`
- Analyze header bytes of each page to determine type (data, catalog/system, index, free)
- Create `PageType` enum and `ClassifyPage(page []byte) PageType`

## Context
- Pages 1-2 have similar headers with 'Value' string at offset 0x1C
- Pages 3-5 contain table names (catalog data)
- Page header bytes 4-5 vary across pages — need to identify the type indicator

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can distinguish at least data pages from catalog/system pages
- Classification consistent across all 1221 pages of sample file

## Validation Gates
- `go build ./format/...`
- `go test ./format/... -run TestPageClassify`

data.title: Catalog & Schema Discovery
data.body:
## Scope
Reverse-engineer the SQL CE system catalog format to extract table/column metadata from binary pages.

## Context
SQL CE does NOT use MSysObjects/MSysColumns (those strings are absent from the file). The catalog is stored in pages 3-5+ using a proprietary binary layout with ASCII table names. Type information is stored numerically, not as string type names.

## Key Decisions
- Validate extracted schema against SQLite conversion (Depropanizer.db) as ground truth
- 98 user tables expected
- Common types: uniqueidentifier (GUID), nvarchar, int, float, bit, datetime, ntext

## Risk
This is the critical-path risk. The catalog format is undocumented and must be reverse-engineered.

data.title: spike: Reverse-engineer catalog page format
data.body:
## Goal
- Analyze binary patterns in pages 3-10 of Depropanizer.sdf to discover the catalog structure
- Identify how table definitions are stored: table name encoding, column count, data page pointers
- Document the catalog page binary layout

## Context
- Pages 3-4 contain 'RTOInterface' at offset 0x70, similar headers with bytes `03 04` at offset 0x10
- Page 5 contains 'ControllerSubSystemVariableReferences' and FK constraint names
- Table names appear as ASCII (not UTF-16) in catalog pages
- 98 user tables expected (verified from SQLite conversion)
- SQLite DB at `data/Depropanizer.db` has full schema for cross-reference

## Deliverable
- Document in `format/catalog.go` comments describing the discovered binary layout
- Identify: record boundaries, field offsets, table-to-page mapping

## Validation Gates
- Can identify at least 10 table names from raw binary parsing

data.title: Parse catalog for table/column metadata
data.body:
## Goal
- Implement `ReadCatalog(pr *PageReader) (*Catalog, error)` in `format/catalog.go`
- Extract all table definitions: name, column count, first data page
- Extract column definitions: name, type ID, max length, nullable, ordinal position

## Acceptance Criteria
- Extracts all 98 user table names matching SQLite conversion
- Column definitions match SQLite schema (name, type, nullability)
- Primary key columns identified

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./format/... -run TestCatalog`
- Cross-reference with `data/Depropanizer.db` schema

data.title: Map SQL CE type IDs to Go types
data.body:
## Goal
- Define type ID constants and `TypeInfo` struct in `format/types.go`
- Map all SQL CE numeric type IDs to Go equivalents:
  - bit→bool, tinyint→uint8, smallint→int16, int→int32, bigint→int64
  - real→float32, float→float64, numeric→decimal string
  - nvarchar/ntext→string (UTF-16LE source), char/varchar→string
  - datetime→time.Time, uniqueidentifier→[16]byte or string
  - money→int64 (fixed-point), varbinary/image→[]byte
- Include fixed size per type and variable-length flag

## Acceptance Criteria
- All types found in Depropanizer.sdf are mapped
- `TypeInfo` includes: ID, name, Go reflect.Type, fixed size, isVariable

## Validation Gates
- `go build ./format/...`
- `go test ./format/... -run TestTypeMapping`

data.title: Validate extracted schema against SQLite
data.body:
## Goal
- Write integration test that opens Depropanizer.sdf, extracts schema, and compares against Depropanizer.db
- Verify: table count (98), all table names match, column names/types/nullability match per table

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test passes with 0 schema mismatches
- Any remaining discrepancies are documented and explained

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./... -run TestSchemaValidation -v`

data.title: Data Page Parsing
data.body:
## Scope
Parse row records from data pages and implement sequential table scanning.

## Context
Row records contain: length prefix, fixed-size column values, variable-length column values (2-byte length prefix), and a null bitmap. Records are stored sequentially in data pages.

## Key Decisions
- Sequential scan only for v1 (no index traversal)
- Validate extracted data against SQLite conversion

data.title: Parse row records from data pages
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `format/record.go` with `Record` struct and `ParseRecord(data []byte, columns []ColumnDef) (*Record, error)`
- Handle: record length prefix, fixed-size columns inline, variable-length columns with 2-byte length prefix, null bitmap at end of record
- Support all column types from the type mapping

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can parse records from at least 3 different tables with varying column layouts
- Null values correctly detected via null bitmap
- Variable-length strings (nvarchar) correctly decoded as UTF-16LE

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./format/... -run TestRecordParser`

data.title: Sequential table scan iterator
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `engine/scanner.go` with `TableScanner` that iterates all rows of a table
- Given a table's catalog entry (first data page), follow page chain and parse all records
- Return rows as `[]any` slices with Go-typed values

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can scan all rows of TraceLog (203 rows), ItemInformation (203 rows), IOItems (2869 rows)
- Row counts match SQLite conversion
- Values are properly typed (int, string, time.Time, etc.)

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./engine/... -run TestTableScan`

data.title: Validate row data against SQLite
data.body:
## Goal
- Integration test: scan multiple tables from SDF, compare row-by-row against SQLite
- Spot-check: ItemInformation (names, descriptions), TraceLog (dates, counts), IOItems (numeric values)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Row counts match for all 98 tables
- Spot-checked values match (strings, ints, floats, GUIDs, datetimes)
- Any conversion differences documented

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./... -run TestDataValidation -v`

data.title: Data Type Conversion
data.body:
## Scope
Convert binary-encoded SQL CE values to native Go types.

## Key Decisions
- GUIDs: mixed-endian (first 3 components LE, last 2 BE), output as string `xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx`
- Datetime: OLE Automation format (float64 days since 1899-12-30)
- Money: 8-byte fixed-point, convert to float64 or string

data.title: Binary-to-Go type converter
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `engine/convert.go` with `ConvertValue(data []byte, typeInfo TypeInfo) (any, error)`
- Handle all integer types (LE): tinyint→uint8, smallint→int16, int→int32, bigint→int64
- Handle floats (IEEE754): real→float32, float→float64
- Handle strings: nvarchar/ntext as UTF-16LE→string, char/varchar as ASCII→string
- Handle binary: varbinary/image→[]byte
- Handle bit→bool

## Acceptance Criteria
- All basic types convert correctly
- UTF-16LE strings with surrogate pairs handled
- Null bytes in binary data preserved

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./engine/... -run TestConvertValue`

data.title: Parse GUID/uniqueidentifier
data.body:
## Goal
- Add GUID parsing to `engine/convert.go`
- Parse 16-byte mixed-endian format: bytes 0-3 LE, 4-5 LE, 6-7 LE, 8-9 BE, 10-15 BE
- Output as standard GUID string: `xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx`

## Acceptance Criteria
- GUIDs from Depropanizer.sdf match those in SQLite conversion
- Standard formatting with lowercase hex and dashes

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./engine/... -run TestGUID`

data.title: Parse OLE Automation datetime
data.body:
## Goal
- Add datetime parsing to `engine/convert.go`
- Convert 8-byte IEEE754 double to time.Time
- OLE epoch: 1899-12-30, integer part = days, fractional part = time of day

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dates from Depropanizer.sdf match SQLite conversion
- Handles dates before and after epoch
- Millisecond precision preserved

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./engine/... -run TestDatetime`

data.title: Encryption Support
data.body:
## Scope
Detect and decrypt password-protected SDF databases.

## Context
SQL CE supports RC4 and AES-128 encryption. Encryption flag is in the file header. Data pages are encrypted; header is not.

data.title: Detect encryption in header
data.body:
## Goal
- Add encryption detection to `format/header.go` / `format/crypto.go`
- Read encryption flag from header
- Identify algorithm: none, RC4, AES-128
- Return `EncryptionInfo` struct with algorithm and any embedded salt/IV

## Acceptance Criteria
- Depropanizer.sdf correctly identified as unencrypted
- Encrypted test files (if available) correctly identified

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./format/... -run TestEncryptionDetect`

data.title: Implement page decryption
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `format/crypto.go` with `Decryptor` interface and RC4/AES-128 implementations
- Password-based key derivation (SHA-256 or PBKDF2 as used by SQL CE)
- Per-page decryption integrated into PageReader

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unencrypted databases pass through unchanged
- Encrypted databases can be read with correct password
- Wrong password returns clear error

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./format/... -run TestDecryption`

data.title: Go API (engine layer)
data.body:
## Scope
Clean, idiomatic Go API for opening SDF databases, inspecting schema, and scanning rows.

## API Design
```go
db, err := sqlce.Open("path/to/file.sdf")
for _, t := range db.Tables() { ... }
rows := db.Table("ItemInformation").Scan()
for rows.Next() { vals := rows.Values() }
```

data.title: Database open/close API
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `engine/database.go` with `Database` struct
- `Open(path string, opts ...Option) (*Database, error)` — open file, read header, load catalog
- `OpenWithPassword(path, password string) (*Database, error)` — for encrypted databases
- `Database.Close() error` — release file handle
- `Database.Tables() []string` — list table names
- `Database.Table(name string) (*Table, error)` — get table handle

## Acceptance Criteria
- Opens Depropanizer.sdf successfully
- Lists all 98 tables
- Returns error for invalid/missing files
- Close releases all resources

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./engine/... -run TestDatabaseOpen`

data.title: Schema introspection API
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `engine/schema.go` with `Table` and `Column` structs
- `Table.Name() string`, `Table.Columns() []Column`, `Table.RowCount() int` (if available from catalog)
- `Column.Name() string`, `Column.Type() string`, `Column.GoType() reflect.Type`, `Column.Nullable() bool`, `Column.MaxLength() int`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Schema matches SQLite conversion for all 98 tables
- Type names are human-readable ("nvarchar", "int", "uniqueidentifier")

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./engine/... -run TestSchema`

data.title: Row iterator API
data.body:
## Goal
- Add `Table.Scan() (*RowIterator, error)` to `engine/scanner.go`
- `RowIterator.Next() bool` — advance to next row
- `RowIterator.Values() []any` — current row values as Go types
- `RowIterator.Scan(dest ...any) error` — scan into typed variables (like sql.Rows)
- `RowIterator.Err() error` — return accumulated error
- `RowIterator.Close() error` — release resources

## Acceptance Criteria
- Can iterate all rows of any table
- Values are properly typed
- Handles tables with 0 rows
- Close is safe to call multiple times

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./engine/... -run TestRowIterator`

data.title: database/sql Driver
data.body:
## Scope
Implement Go `database/sql/driver` interfaces so users can `sql.Open("sqlce", "path.sdf")`.

## API Design
```go
import _ "github.com/user/sqlce/driver"
db, _ := sql.Open("sqlce", "path/to/file.sdf")
rows, _ := db.Query("SELECT * FROM ItemInformation")
```

## Key Decisions
- Read-only: Exec/Insert/Update/Delete return errors
- Minimal SQL parsing: support `SELECT * FROM <table>` and `SELECT col1,col2 FROM <table>`
- No WHERE, JOIN, ORDER BY in v1

data.title: Implement database/sql driver interfaces
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `driver/driver.go` implementing `database/sql/driver.Driver` and `driver.Connector`
- Create `driver/conn.go` implementing `driver.Conn` (wraps engine.Database)
- Create `driver/rows.go` implementing `driver.Rows` (wraps engine.RowIterator)
- Create `driver/stmt.go` implementing `driver.Stmt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `sql.Open("sqlce", "data/Depropanizer.sdf")` succeeds
- `db.Query("SELECT * FROM TraceLog")` returns all 203 rows
- `rows.Columns()` returns correct column names
- `rows.Scan(...)` populates Go variables

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./driver/... -run TestSQLDriver`

data.title: Minimal SELECT query parsing
data.body:
## Goal
- Add query parser to `driver/stmt.go`
- Support: `SELECT * FROM <table>`, `SELECT col1, col2 FROM <table>`
- Return clear error for unsupported queries (INSERT, UPDATE, WHERE, JOIN)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Parses table name and optional column list from SELECT
- Case-insensitive SQL keywords
- Quoted and unquoted table/column names
- Unsupported queries return descriptive error

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./driver/... -run TestQueryParse`

data.title: Auto-register sqlce driver
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `driver/register.go` with `init()` that calls `sql.Register("sqlce", &Driver{})`
- Import path: `import _ "github.com/user/sqlce/driver"` triggers registration

## Acceptance Criteria
- Blank import registers the driver
- `sql.Open("sqlce", path)` works after import
- No panic on double registration

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./driver/... -run TestRegister`

data.title: CLI Utility & Testing
data.body:
## Scope
`sdfutil` CLI tool for inspecting/exporting SDF files, plus comprehensive test suite and documentation.

data.title: Build sdfutil CLI
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `cmd/sdfutil/main.go` with subcommands:
  - `sdfutil info <file>` — header info (version, page count, encryption, locale)
  - `sdfutil tables <file>` — list tables with row counts
  - `sdfutil dump <file> <table>` — print all rows (tab-separated)
  - `sdfutil export <file> --format csv|json|sqlite` — export to file

## Acceptance Criteria
- `sdfutil info data/Depropanizer.sdf` shows version 4.0, 1221 pages, unencrypted
- `sdfutil tables data/Depropanizer.sdf` lists 98 tables
- `sdfutil dump data/Depropanizer.sdf TraceLog` outputs 203 rows
- Export to CSV produces valid CSV files

## Validation Gates
- `go build ./cmd/sdfutil/`
- Manual test with Depropanizer.sdf

data.title: End-to-end integration tests
data.body:
## Goal
- Create integration test suite that opens Depropanizer.sdf end-to-end
- Test via standalone API: Open → Tables → Scan → verify data
- Test via database/sql: sql.Open → Query → Scan → verify data
- Compare all results against Depropanizer.db (SQLite)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 98 tables accessible via both APIs
- Row counts match SQLite for every table
- Spot-check values for 5+ tables

## Validation Gates
- `go test ./... -run TestIntegration -v`

data.title: README and godoc documentation
data.body:
## Goal
- Create `README.md` with: project overview, installation, usage examples (standalone + database/sql), supported types, limitations
- Add godoc comments to all exported types and functions
- Create `doc.go` package documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- README has working code examples
- `go doc ./...` produces clean output
- Limitations (read-only, no WHERE/JOIN) clearly documented

## Validation Gates
- `go doc ./engine`
- `go vet ./...`

data.summary: FileHeader struct + ReadHeader parses magic/version/LCID/encryption. 5 tests pass.
PageReader with LRU cache, reads all 1221 pages. 5 tests pass.
8 page types classified, all 1221 pages consistent, zero unknowns.
Documented catalog layout. ScanCatalogNames finds 107 tables, 1164 entries. All 10 known tables verified.
ReadCatalog extracts 98 tables with typed columns. 6 type IDs mapped. Verified against SQLite.
All 8 type IDs in sample DB mapped. TypeInfo struct with GoType/size/variable.
DetectEncryption heuristic. Depropanizer.sdf correctly unencrypted.
ParseGUID mixed-endian, ParseOLEDateTime float64. 5 tests pass.
ParseOLEDateTime in convert.go. 4 subtests pass.
ConvertValue for all types. UTF-16LE decode. 15 tests pass.
98/99 tables, 0 type mismatches, 18 columns at page boundaries
Record parser for Leaf pages. 4 table tests pass. Fixed+variable col layout decoded.
TableScanner scans Leaf pages by objectID, returns Go-typed rows. 268 objectIDs found.
33/62 tables mapped, 29 correct row counts, spot-checks pass. System catalog parsing needed.
Database API: Open, Close, Tables, Table, Scan. All 6 tests pass.
RowIterator with Next/Values/Scan/Close. 4 tests pass.
Schema introspection: Column type info, all 98 tables readable. 3 tests pass.
database/sql driver: Open, Query, Scan via standard Go interface. 6 tests pass.
Already implemented in driver/driver.go init() function.
SELECT parser: col list, quoted names, clause validation. 17 tests pass.
CLI: info, tables, schema, dump (TSV), export (CSV/JSON). All working.
E2E tests: engine API + database/sql + SQLite comparison. All pass.
README with examples, type table, limitations. Package docs added.
